rationale: Both evaluations rest on exactly the same reasoning—the candidate is vastly overqualified for a junior/mid-level role, creating salary mismatch and flight-risk concerns. Neither evaluation mentions the caregiving career gap or its duration at all, so the changed detail did not influence the reasoning. The 1-point score drop (3 to 2) is unaccompanied by any new gap-related concern and falls within ordinary run-to-run noise; the strengths and concerns are merely reworded versions of the same points.
